title: Prove Android reduced-motion behavior
goal: Establish a physical, commit-bound Android reduced-motion result for the public globe without treating an unavailable device setting or desktop emulation as a pass.

approval.note: Use an isolated Commonworld worktree. Before merge require a complete downloadable diff, head- and diff-bound self-review, green CI and exact post-merge live validation.

acceptance.assertion: A named physical Android device and Chrome version either expose a reproducible reduced-motion setting path or record the exact absence/blocker; absence cannot be converted into PASS.
With reduced motion genuinely active, camera and panel transitions are measured against an exact commit and use the contracted non-animated path or a documented equivalent.
One-finger globe movement, pinch zoom, focus-panel operation, layer switching and browser-back behavior remain usable under the tested setting.
The physical receipt binds repository, commit, tree, device, browser, setting state, assertions, failures and SHA-256; desktop emulation is only supplementary evidence.
The result states which Android version and device were covered and does not claim all Android devices, screen-reader support, WCAG conformance or future runtime correctness.

operator_disposition.reason: The operator chose not to pursue the Android-specific reduced-motion follow-up.
